info.title: nanoclr
info.description: .NET nanoFramework CLR as a .NET tool. Allows running nanoCLR as a virtual device.

options.name: --clrpath
options.description: Path to the directory from which to load a specific version of nanoFramework.nanoCLR.dll.

options.name: --localinstance
options.description: Path to a local instance of the nanoCLR.

options.name: --verbosity
options.description: Sets the verbosity level of the command. Allowed values are q[uiet], m[inimal], n[ormal], d[etailed], and diag[nostic]. Not supported in every command; see specific command page to determine if this option is available. [default: n]

options.name: --assemblies
options.description: List of PE assemblies to load in nanoCLR.

options.name: --serialport
options.description: Virtual serial port exposing the nanoCLR instance.

options.name: --networkport
options.description: Set TCP/IP port exposing the nanoCLR instance.

options.name: --host
options.description: Set TCP/IP host address exposing the nanoCLR instance.

options.name: --broadcast
options.description: Set UDP broadcast port for device discovery.

options.name: --namedpipe
options.description: Set Pipe Name exposing the nanoCLR instance.

options.name: --tracewireprotocol
options.description: Trace wire protocol packets.

options.name: --profiler
options.description: Enable profiler. [default: False]

options.name: --dumpprofilerdata
options.description: Dump profiler raw data to a file. [default: False]

options.name: --resolve
options.description: Tries to resolve cross-assembly references.

options.name: --monitorparentpid
options.description: Parent process id to monitor - exit if the parent exits.

options.name: --waitfordebugger
options.description: Option to wait for a debugger connection after nanoCLR is started. [default: False]

options.name: --loopafterexit
options.description: Option to remain in loop waiting for a debugger connection after the program exits. [default: False]

options.name: --forcegc
options.description: Option to force GC before each allocation. [default: False]

options.name: --compactionaftergc
options.description: Option to force heap compaction after each GC run. [default: False]

options.name: --help
options.description: Display this help screen.

options.name: --version
options.description: Display version information.

commands.name: instance
commands.description: Operations with the current nanoCLR instance.

options.name: --clrpath
options.description: Path to the directory from which to load a specific version of nanoFramework.nanoCLR.dll.

options.name: --localinstance
options.description: Path to a local instance of the nanoCLR.

options.name: --verbosity
options.description: Sets the verbosity level of the command. Allowed values are q[uiet], m[inimal], n[ormal], d[etailed], and diag[nostic]. Not supported in every command; see specific command page to determine if this option is available. [default: n]

options.name: --getversion
options.description: Gets the version of the current nanoCLR instance.

options.name: --getnativeassemblies
options.description: Gets the names and versions of the native assemblies.

options.name: --update
options.description: Checks for stable versions of nanoCLR and updates it, if there is one. A specific version can be specified. To check preview versions add the --preview option. [default: False]

options.name: --preview
options.description: Include preview versions when checking for nanoCLR updates.

options.name: --clrversion
options.description: Specify a version of nanoCRL to install.

options.name: --help
options.description: Display this help screen.

options.name: --version
options.description: Display version information.

options.name: --list
options.description: List existing Virtual Bridges

options.name: --create
options.description: Create Virtual Serial Device

options.name: --remove
options.description: Remove Virtual Serial Device

options.name: --install
options.description: Install Virtual Serial Port Tools [default: False]

options.name: --verbosity
options.description: Sets the verbosity level of the command. Allowed values are q[uiet], m[inimal], n[ormal], d[etailed], and diag[nostic]. Not supported in every command; see specific command page to determine if this option is available. [default: d]
